{"key_event":"CORFO developmental state and Popular Front mobilization","reasoning":"CORFO, reconstruction after the Chillán earthquake, wartime management, social policy, and emergency powers expanded an already strong state’s administrative and coercive reach, though inflation and rural oligarchic limits kept it below overwhelming. Civil society remained strong through unions, parties, elections, and Popular Front/left participation in government, but its net gain was limited by rural dependency, labor-left fragmentation, and the 1947 repression of Communist-led labor.","society_change":0.2,"society_power":7.7,"state_change":0.4,"state_power":8.7}
